meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Pricing Strategies to Finish Your Projects
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Master SaaS pricing strategies to validate your MVP faster using Antigravity and Claude Code. Build, launch, and monetize today.
content.answer_block: You abandon projects because your scope lacks a revenue anchor. By implementing a tiered pricing model before writing core features, you force market validation. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing infrastructure and Claude Code to automate the integration, ensuring your MVP is built around a profitable core, not endless features.
content.intro:
You know the cycle: a flash of brilliance, a week of intense coding, and then the crushing weight of scope creep. You are not failing because you lack technical skill; you are failing because you are building features instead of a business. Without a pricing model, you have no North Star to prioritize the work that actually matters.

True velocity comes from constraints. By treating your SaaS pricing as the architectural blueprint of your application, you transform a vague idea into a commercial entity. This approach forces you to cut the 'nice-to-haves' and focus exclusively on the high-value features your users are willing to pay for, effectively killing the urge to abandon your build.

steps.title: Define Your Value Tiers
steps.description: Map your features to three distinct pricing tiers: Starter, Growth, and Scale. This forces you to identify the 'killer feature' that justifies the mid-tier price point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SaaS pricing structure for a B2B AI tool. Define 3 tiers with feature gating logic that aligns with standard industry SaaS metrics.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a data schema for a multi-tier subscription model using Prisma and PostgreSQL.'

steps.title: Architect the Billing Logic
steps.description: Implement the core logic that restricts access based on subscription status. This creates a technical 'fence' around your product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function for an Express.js app that checks user subscription status against a Stripe-compatible database schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/middleware/auth.ts --instructions 'Inject subscription tier validation logic into the request pipeline.'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Checkout
steps.description: Move beyond fake buttons. Connect real payment processing to ensure that your project has a functional path to revenue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Stripe checkout session handler in TypeScript that maps to the tiers defined in step one.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a secure Stripe webhook handler to manage subscription lifecycle events.'

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating
steps.description: Use decorators or conditional rendering to lock premium features. This makes the value proposition tangible to the user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a feature flag utility that toggles UI components based on the user's active subscription tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/components/Dashboard.tsx --instructions 'Wrap premium chart components with a subscription gating check.'

steps.title: Automate Deployment and Validation
steps.description: Finalize your MVP by deploying to a live environment where you can test the pricing flow with actual users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a production-ready CI/CD configuration for a Next.js app that includes environment variable management for Stripe keys.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Verify all billing routes and deploy to Vercel via CLI.'

pro_tips: Always price your lowest tier high enough to filter out 'tire-kickers' who create support debt.
Use 'Feature Gating' as a tool to prioritize your development roadmap; if a feature isn't worth a tier upgrade, it's not worth building yet.
Implement a 'Usage-Based' cap early, even if it's simple, to prepare your infrastructure for scaling.
Include a 'Contact Us' tier for enterprise, which forces you to think about the high-end value of your product.
Never build an MVP without a 'Pricing' page; the page itself is the most important component of your application.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing flow is live, spend 48 hours getting your first 10 users to sign up. If they won't pay, pivot the feature set before adding more code.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark. Align your code with your revenue model and watch your project abandonment rate drop to zero. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
